lead: AI continues to shape industries and society, raising questions about governance, environmental impact, and security.

stories.title: From test case to control tower: How DXC and ServiceNow are governing enterprise AI at scale
stories.summary: As AI technologies become integral to business operations, the governance of these systems is emerging as a critical challenge. DXC and ServiceNow are at the forefront, developing frameworks that ensure AI is used responsibly and effectively across enterprises. This shift is essential as organizations grapple with the complexities of AI decision-making.

stories.title: Scotland’s ‘green datacentres’ policy ignores emissions impact of AI, analysis shows
stories.summary: A recent analysis reveals that Scotland's initiative to promote green datacentres may overlook the significant carbon emissions associated with AI technologies. The policy, aimed at attracting AI investments, could inadvertently lead to increased environmental impact, highlighting the need for a more comprehensive approach to sustainability in tech.

stories.title: Everyone is navigating AI security in real time — even Google
stories.summary: As AI technology evolves, so do the security challenges associated with it. Major players like Google are actively working to address these vulnerabilities in real time. The ongoing adjustments reflect the broader industry's struggle to keep pace with the rapid advancements in AI capabilities.

stories.title: AI washing: firms are scrambling to rebrand themselves as tech-focused
stories.summary: In a bid to capitalize on the AI hype, many companies are rebranding themselves as AI specialists, even when their technologies do not involve advanced AI. This trend, termed 'AI washing', raises concerns about authenticity and the potential for misleading consumers and investors.

stories.title: McKinsey built a free AI tool so candidates stop paying $500/hour interview coaches
stories.summary: McKinsey has launched a free AI tool designed to help job candidates practice for interviews without the hefty price tag of traditional coaching services. This initiative aims to democratize access to interview preparation, leveling the playing field for candidates from diverse backgrounds.

stories.title: The AI security gap nobody wants to admit is already here
stories.summary: The accidental public release of Anthropic's Claude Code has exposed significant vulnerabilities in AI security protocols. This incident highlights the urgent need for robust security measures as AI technologies become more prevalent and integrated into various sectors.

stories.title: AI's new cottage industry: Lawyers defending students accused of cheating
stories.summary: With the rise of AI tools, accusations of cheating among students have surged, leading to a new niche for legal professionals. Lawyers are now stepping in to defend students facing allegations of AI-assisted academic dishonesty, underscoring the complex intersection of technology and education.

stories.title: Hackers are learning to exploit chatbot ‘personalities’
stories.summary: As AI chatbots become more sophisticated, hackers are finding new ways to exploit their vulnerabilities. This trend raises alarms about the security of AI systems and the potential for misuse, emphasizing the need for ongoing improvements in AI safety and ethics.

stories.title: You can snag a 2TB Corsair SSD for 60% off at Best Buy - and it gets my approval
stories.summary: In the realm of AI and data management, storage solutions are critical. Best Buy is offering significant discounts on high-capacity SSDs, which are essential for handling the large datasets AI applications require. This deal highlights the importance of robust storage in the AI landscape.
